name: Braised Beef with Brandy and Mustard
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 lb Bottom round steak (trimmed of fat and membrane)
4 Sun-dried tomatoes (not oil-packed), cut into slivers
3 large Garlic cloves, peeled and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. In a Dutch oven or deep skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the meat and brown it on all sides. Transfer the meat to a plate lined with paper towels and drain off any excess fat in the pan. Return the pan to the heat and immediately add brandy, stirring to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom. Cook until the brandy reduces to a syrupy glaze.
3. Stir in stock, mustards, shallots, sun-dried tomatoes, garlic, juniper berries, and bay leaf. Bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Return the meat to the pan, cover tightly, and place in the oven. Bake for 1½-2 hours, turning the meat every 30 minutes, or until very tender.
5. Remove the bay leaf and season the sauce with black pepper. Cut the meat into thin slices, arrange on a platter, and spoon the sauce over.
